2604.13551.title: Debate to Align: Reliable Entity Alignment through Two-Stage Multi-Agent Debate
2604.13551.paper_summary: Entity alignment (EA) aims to identify entities referring to the same real-world object across different knowledge graphs (KGs). Recent approaches based on large language models (LLMs) typically obtain entity embeddings through knowledge representation learning and use embedding similarity to identify an alignment-uncertain entity set. For each uncertain entity, a candidate entity set (CES) is then retrieved based on embedding similarity to support subsequent alignment reasoning and decision making. However, the reliability of the CES and the reasoning capability of LLMs critically affect the effectiveness of subsequent alignment decisions. To address this issue, we propose AgentEA, a reliable EA framework based on multi-agent debate. AgentEA first improves embedding quality through entity representation preference optimization, and then introduces a two-stage multi-role debate mechanism consisting of lightweight debate verification and deep debate alignment to progressively enhance the reliability of alignment decisions while enabling more efficient debate-based reasoning. Extensive experiments on public benchmarks under cross-lingual, sparse, large-scale, and heterogeneous settings demonstrate the effectiveness of AgentEA.
2604.13551.paper_summary_zh: 實體對齊（EA）旨在識別不同知識圖譜（KGs）中指向相同現實世界對象的實體。最近基於大型語言模型（LLMs）的方法通常通過知識表示學習獲得實體嵌入，並使用嵌入相似度來識別對齊不確定的實體集。對於每個不確定的實體，然後根據嵌入相似度檢索候選實體集（CES），以支持後續的對齊推理和決策。然而，CES的可靠性和LLMs的推理能力對後續對齊決策的有效性有著至關重要的影響。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了AgentEA，一個基於多代理辯論的可靠EA框架。AgentEA首先通過實體表示偏好優化來提高嵌入質量，然後引入一個由輕量級辯論驗證和深度辯論對齊組成的兩階段多角色辯論機制，以逐步增強對齊決策的可靠性，同時實現更高效的基於辯論的推理。在跨語言、稀疏、大規模和異構設置下的公共基準上進行的大量實驗證明了AgentEA的有效性。
2604.13551.author: Cunda Wang et.al.
2604.13551.authors: Cunda Wang, Ziying Ma, Po Hu, Weihua Wang, Feilong Bao
